name: Individual Brain Charting (IBC)
description: A deep-phenotyping fMRI dataset in which a fixed cohort of 12 participants each performed dozens of cognitive tasks at high spatial resolution (1.5 mm), yielding hundreds of functional contrasts per person. The task battery includes social-cognition and theory-of-mind paradigms (e.g., false-belief and animated social-interaction tasks), making IBC a precise within-subject reference for localizing the social brain.
citation: Pinho AL, Amadon A, Gauthier B, et al. (2020). Individual Brain Charting dataset extension, second release of high-resolution fMRI data for cognitive mapping. Scientific Data, 7, 353.
